title: Pilosella leucotricha
intro: Pilosella leucotricha, also known as a type of hawkweed, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to Europe and parts of Asia. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to its bioactive constituents, such as sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella leucotricha, including sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, have been reported to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, contributing to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pilosella leucotricha is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or extract, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
taxonomy: Pilosella leucotricha is classified in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It belongs to the genus Pilosella, which includes about 100 species of flowering plants. The genus Pilosella is further divided into several sections, but the exact classification of Pilosella leucotricha within these sections is not specified. Pilosella leucotricha is also sometimes classified as Senecio leucotrichus, as some sources place it in the genus Senecio. However, the correct classification may vary depending on the taxonomic source.
